Before vs after

PDF accessibility check

PDFs before and after remediation. Click on each file to see what was failing and what was fixed.

Each PDF was checked against PDF/UA-1 (ISO 14289-1) — The international standard for accessible PDFs. A passing PDF can be used by people who rely on screen readers. ‘Before’ is the original; ‘after’ is what came out of the remediator.

Before folder
/Users/umar/projects/brief/src/brief/cogent-binaries
After folder
/Users/umar/projects/brief/src/brief/cogent-binaries_remediated
Standard
PDF/UA-1 (ISO 14289-1) (/Users/umar/projects/veraPDF-validation-profiles/PDF_UA/PDFUA-1.xml)
Compared
268 files (matched by basename)
268Before remediation6Improved but still failing260Now passes2Removed
PDFs passing
0 → 260 / 268
260 more passing
Rule violations
2,572,599 → 32
2,572,567 fewer

260 of 268 PDFs meet PDF/UA-1 (ISO 14289-1). 6 still fail.

What was failing before268 files

Original state of the ‘before’ folder — 268 files were failing the standard. What changed after remediation is in the sections that follow.

12 rules failing
10k_report_2021.pdf
Was failing 12 rules (628,219 violations).
Rules 12Violations 628,219Successful checks 1,965
Rule-by-rule detail
  • ✗
    FailingMissing PDF/UA marker1 violation
    The PDF didn't declare itself as following the PDF/UA accessibility standard.
    5-t1 The PDF/UA version and conformance level of a file shall be specified using the PDF/UA Identification extension schema
  • ✗
    FailingThe document catalog dictionary shall include a MarkInfo dictionary containin…1 violation
    The document catalog dictionary shall include a MarkInfo dictionary containing an entry, Marked, whose value shall be true
    6.2-t1 The document catalog dictionary shall include a MarkInfo dictionary containing an entry, Marked, whose value shall be true
  • ✗
    FailingThe document catalog dictionary shall include a ViewerPreferences dictionary …1 violation
    The document catalog dictionary shall include a ViewerPreferences dictionary containing a DisplayDocTitle key, whose value shall be true
    7.1-t10 The document catalog dictionary shall include a ViewerPreferences dictionary containing a DisplayDocTitle key, whose value shall be true
  • ✗
    FailingThe logical structure of the conforming file shall be described by a structur…1 violation
    The logical structure of the conforming file shall be described by a structure hierarchy rooted in the StructTreeRoot entry of the document catalog dictionary, as described in ISO 32000-1:2008, 14.7
    7.1-t11 The logical structure of the conforming file shall be described by a structure hierarchy rooted in the StructTreeRoot entry of the document catalog dictionary, as described in ISO 32000-1:2008, 14.7
  • ✗
    FailingUntagged content315,597 violations
    Visible text or graphics weren't labelled — invisible to screen readers.
    7.1-t3 Content shall be marked as Artifact or tagged as real content
  • ✗
    FailingMissing document title1 violation
    The PDF had no human-readable title in its metadata.
    7.1-t9 The Metadata stream in the document's catalog dictionary shall contain a dc:title entry, where dc is the recommended prefix for the Dublin Core metadata schema as defined in the XMP specification, which clearly identifies the document
  • ✗
    FailingAnnotation without alt text123 violations
    Clickable areas (links, form fields, etc.) had no description for screen-reader users.
    7.18.1-t2 An annotation (except Widget annotations or hidden annotations, or those having rectangle outside the crop-box) shall have either Contents key or an Alt entry in the enclosing structure element
  • ✗
    FailingEvery page on which there is an annotation shall contain in its page dictiona…70 violations
    Every page on which there is an annotation shall contain in its page dictionary the key Tabs, and its value shall be S
    7.18.3-t1 Every page on which there is an annotation shall contain in its page dictionary the key Tabs, and its value shall be S
  • ✗
    FailingUntagged hyperlinks123 violations
    Hyperlinks weren't marked up as links in the accessibility tree.
    7.18.5-t1 Links shall be tagged according to ISO 32000-1:2008, 14.8.4.4.2, Link Element
  • ✗
    FailingHyperlinks without description123 violations
    Hyperlinks had no accessible text describing where they go.
    7.18.5-t2 Links shall contain an alternate description via their Contents key as described in ISO 32000-1:2008, 14.9.3
  • ✗
    FailingNatural language for text in page content shall be determined312,169 violations
    Natural language for text in page content shall be determined
    7.2-t34 Natural language for text in page content shall be determined
  • ✗
    FailingThe Unicode values specified in the ToUnicode CMap shall all be greater than …9 violations
    The Unicode values specified in the ToUnicode CMap shall all be greater than zero (0), but not equal to either U+FEFF or U+FFFE
    7.21.7-t2 The Unicode values specified in the ToUnicode CMap shall all be greater than zero (0), but not equal to either U+FEFF or U+FFFE
Failing 628,219 violations

    FailingUntagged content11,942 violations
    Visible text or graphics weren't labelled — invisible to screen readers.
    7.1-t3 Content shall be marked as Artifact or tagged as real content
  • ✗
    FailingFont CIDSet incomplete5 violations
    An embedded CID font's CIDSet didn't list all character IDs in the font.
    7.21.4.2-t2 If the FontDescriptor dictionary of an embedded CID font contains a CIDSet stream, then it shall identify all CIDs which are present in the font program, regardless of whether a CID in the font is referenced or used by the PDF or not

    FailingFont CIDSet incomplete2 violations

    7.3-t1 Figure tags shall include an alternative representation or replacement text that represents the contents marked with the Figure tag as noted in ISO 32000-1:2008, 14.7.2, Table 323

Rules 4Violations 8Successful checks 52,969
Rule-by-rule detail
  • ✗
    FailingThe document catalog dictionary shall include a ViewerPreferences dictionary …1 violation
    The document catalog dictionary shall include a ViewerPreferences dictionary containing a DisplayDocTitle key, whose value shall be true
    7.1-t10 The document catalog dictionary shall include a ViewerPreferences dictionary containing a DisplayDocTitle key, whose value shall be true
  • ✗
    FailingThe Catalog dictionary of a conforming file shall contain the Metadata key wh…1 violation
    The Catalog dictionary of a conforming file shall contain the Metadata key whose value is a metadata stream as defined in ISO 32000-1:2008, 14.3.2. The metadata stream dictionary shall contain entry Type with value /Metadata and entry Subtype with value /XML
    7.1-t8 The Catalog dictionary of a conforming file shall contain the Metadata key whose value is a metadata stream as defined in ISO 32000-1:2008, 14.3.2. The metadata stream dictionary shall contain entry Type with value /Metadata and entry Subtype with value /XML
  • ✗
    FailingTR element may contain only TH and TD elements3 violations
    TR element may contain only TH and TD elements
    7.2-t10 TR element may contain only TH and TD elements
  • ✗
    FailingFont not embedded3 violations
    A font used by the PDF wasn't embedded — it may render incorrectly on other systems.
    7.21.4.1-t1 The font programs for all fonts used for rendering within a conforming file shall be embedded within that file, as defined in ISO 32000-1:2008, 9.9

    FailingContent mixed with decoration4 violations

  • ✗
    FailingNon-standard tag without mapping4 violations
    Custom structure tags were used without being mapped to a standard equivalent.
    7.1-t5 All non-standard structure types shall be mapped to the nearest functionally equivalent standard type, as defined in ISO 32000-1:2008, 14.8.4, in the role map dictionary of the structure tree root. This mapping may be indirect; within the role map a non-standard type can map directly to another non-standard type, but eventually the mapping shall terminate at a standard type
  • ✗

  • ✗
    FailingMissing PDF/UA marker1 violation
    The PDF didn't declare itself as following the PDF/UA accessibility standard.
    5-t1 The PDF/UA version and conformance level of a file shall be specified using the PDF/UA Identification extension schema
  • ✗
    FailingDecoration mixed with content6 violations
    Decorative artwork was incorrectly tagged as real content.
    7.1-t1 Content marked as Artifact should not be present inside tagged content
  • ✗
    FailingThe document catalog dictionary shall include a ViewerPreferences dictionary …1 violation
    The document catalog dictionary shall include a ViewerPreferences dictionary containing a DisplayDocTitle key, whose value shall be true
    7.1-t10 The document catalog dictionary shall include a ViewerPreferences dictionary containing a DisplayDocTitle key, whose value shall be true
  • ✗
    FailingContent mixed with decoration6 violations
    Real content was incorrectly tagged as decoration.
    7.1-t2 Tagged content should not be present inside content marked as Artifact
  • ✗
    FailingNon-standard tag without mapping6 violations
    Custom structure tags were used without being mapped to a standard equivalent.
    7.1-t5 All non-standard structure types shall be mapped to the nearest functionally equivalent standard type, as defined in ISO 32000-1:2008, 14.8.4, in the role map dictionary of the structure tree root. This mapping may be indirect; within the role map a non-standard type can map directly to another non-standard type, but eventually the mapping shall terminate at a standard type
  • ✗
    FailingLayer config missing name1 violation
    An optional-content (layer) configuration was missing its name.
    7.10-t1 Each optional content configuration dictionary that forms the value of the D key, or that is an element in the array that forms the value of the Configs key in the OCProperties dictionary, shall contain the Name key
  • ✗
    FailingTable rows shall have the same number of columns (taking into account column …4 violations
    Table rows shall have the same number of columns (taking into account column spans)
    7.2-t42 Table rows shall have the same number of columns (taking into account column spans)
  • ✗
    FailingTable rows shall have the same number of columns (taking into account column …1 violation
    Table rows shall have the same number of columns (taking into account column spans)
    7.2-t43 Table rows shall have the same number of columns (taking into account column spans)
  • ✗
    FailingFont CIDSet incomplete2 violations
    An embedded CID font's CIDSet didn't list all character IDs in the font.
    7.21.4.2-t2 If the FontDescriptor dictionary of an embedded CID font contains a CIDSet stream, then it shall identify all CIDs which are present in the font program, regardless of whether a CID in the font is referenced or used by the PDF or not
  • ✗
    FailingFigure tags shall include an alternative representation or replacement text t…99 violations
    Figure tags shall include an alternative representation or replacement text that represents the contents marked with the Figure tag as noted in ISO 32000-1:2008, 14.7.2, Table 323
    7.3-t1 Figure tags shall include an alternative representation or replacement text that represents the contents marked with the Figure tag as noted in ISO 32000-1:2008, 14.7.2, Table 323
  • ✗
    FailingFor documents that are not strongly structured, as described in ISO 32000-1:2…1 violation
    For documents that are not strongly structured, as described in ISO 32000-1:2008, 14.8.4.3.5, heading tags shall be used as follows: (*) If any heading tags are used, H1 shall be the first. (*) A document may use more than one instance of any specific tag level. For example, a tag level may be repeated if document content requires it. (*) If document semantics require a descending sequence of headers, such a sequence shall proceed in strict numerical order and shall not skip an intervening heading level. (*) A document may increment its heading sequence without restarting at H1 if document semantics require it
    7.4.2-t1 For documents that are not strongly structured, as described in ISO 32000-1:2008, 14.8.4.3.5, heading tags shall be used as follows: (*) If any heading tags are used, H1 shall be the first. (*) A document may use more than one instance of any specific tag level. For example, a tag level may be repeated if document content requires it. (*) If document semantics require a descending sequence of headers, such a sequence shall proceed in strict numerical order and shall not skip an intervening heading level. (*) A document may increment its heading sequence without restarting at H1 if document semantics require it
  • ✗
    FailingTable headers without scope3 violations
    Table-header cells didn't say whether they apply to a row or a column.
    7.5-t1 If the table's structure is not determinable via Headers and IDs, then structure elements of type TH shall have a Scope attribute

    FailingNon-standard tag without mapping5 violations
    Custom structure tags were used without being mapped to a standard equivalent.
    7.1-t5 All non-standard structure types shall be mapped to the nearest functionally equivalent standard type, as defined in ISO 32000-1:2008, 14.8.4, in the role map dictionary of the structure tree root. This mapping may be indirect; within the role map a non-standard type can map directly to another non-standard type, but eventually the mapping shall terminate at a standard type

    FailingFigure tags shall include an alternative representation or replacement text t…79 violations
    Figure tags shall include an alternative representation or replacement text that represents the contents marked with the Figure tag as noted in ISO 32000-1:2008, 14.7.2, Table 323
    7.3-t1 Figure tags shall include an alternative representation or replacement text that represents the contents marked with the Figure tag as noted in ISO 32000-1:2008, 14.7.2, Table 323
  • ✗
    FailingFor documents that are not strongly structured, as described in ISO 32000-1:2…1 violation
    For documents that are not strongly structured, as described in ISO 32000-1:2008, 14.8.4.3.5, heading tags shall be used as follows: (*) If any heading tags are used, H1 shall be the first. (*) A document may use more than one instance of any specific tag level. For example, a tag level may be repeated if document content requires it. (*) If document semantics require a descending sequence of headers, such a sequence shall proceed in strict numerical order and shall not skip an intervening heading level. (*) A document may increment its heading sequence without restarting at H1 if document semantics require it
    7.4.2-t1 For documents that are not strongly structured, as described in ISO 32000-1:2008, 14.8.4.3.5, heading tags shall be used as follows: (*) If any heading tags are used, H1 shall be the first. (*) A document may use more than one instance of any specific tag level. For example, a tag level may be repeated if document content requires it. (*) If document semantics require a descending sequence of headers, such a sequence shall proceed in strict numerical order and shall not skip an intervening heading level. (*) A document may increment its heading sequence without restarting at H1 if document semantics require it
  • ✗
    FailingTable headers without scope8 violations
    Table-header cells didn't say whether they apply to a row or a column.
    7.5-t1 If the table's structure is not determinable via Headers and IDs, then structure elements of type TH shall have a Scope attribute

  • ✗
    FailingFigure tags shall include an alternative representation or replacement text t…1 violation

  • ✗
    FailingFor documents that are not strongly structured, as described in ISO 32000-1:2…2 violations
    For documents that are not strongly structured, as described in ISO 32000-1:2008, 14.8.4.3.5, heading tags shall be used as follows: (*) If any heading tags are used, H1 shall be the first. (*) A document may use more than one instance of any specific tag level. For example, a tag level may be repeated if document content requires it. (*) If document semantics require a descending sequence of headers, such a sequence shall proceed in strict numerical order and shall not skip an intervening heading level. (*) A document may increment its heading sequence without restarting at H1 if document semantics require it
    7.4.2-t1 For documents that are not strongly structured, as described in ISO 32000-1:2008, 14.8.4.3.5, heading tags shall be used as follows: (*) If any heading tags are used, H1 shall be the first. (*) A document may use more than one instance of any specific tag level. For example, a tag level may be repeated if document content requires it. (*) If document semantics require a descending sequence of headers, such a sequence shall proceed in strict numerical order and shall not skip an intervening heading level. (*) A document may increment its heading sequence without restarting at H1 if document semantics require it

    FailingThe document catalog dictionary shall include a ViewerPreferences dictionary …1 violation
    The document catalog dictionary shall include a ViewerPreferences dictionary containing a DisplayDocTitle key, whose value shall be true
    7.1-t10 The document catalog dictionary shall include a ViewerPreferences dictionary containing a DisplayDocTitle key, whose value shall be true
  • ✗
    FailingThe Catalog dictionary of a conforming file shall contain the Metadata key wh…1 violation
    The Catalog dictionary of a conforming file shall contain the Metadata key whose value is a metadata stream as defined in ISO 32000-1:2008, 14.3.2. The metadata stream dictionary shall contain entry Type with value /Metadata and entry Subtype with value /XML
    7.1-t8 The Catalog dictionary of a conforming file shall contain the Metadata key whose value is a metadata stream as defined in ISO 32000-1:2008, 14.3.2. The metadata stream dictionary shall contain entry Type with value /Metadata and entry Subtype with value /XML
  • ✗
    FailingAnnotation without alt text5 violations
    Clickable areas (links, form fields, etc.) had no description for screen-reader users.
    7.18.1-t2 An annotation (except Widget annotations or hidden annotations, or those having rectangle outside the crop-box) shall have either Contents key or an Alt entry in the enclosing structure element
  • ✗
    FailingHyperlinks without description5 violations
    Hyperlinks had no accessible text describing where they go.
    7.18.5-t2 Links shall contain an alternate description via their Contents key as described in ISO 32000-1:2008, 14.9.3

    TR element may contain only TH and TD elements

    7.21.4.1-t1 The font programs for all fonts used for rendering within a conforming file shall be embedded within that file, as defined in ISO 32000-1:2008, 9.9
  • ✗
    FailingFigure tags shall include an alternative representation or replacement text t…1 violation

    FailingFont not embedded6 violations
    A font used by the PDF wasn't embedded — it may render incorrectly on other systems.

  • ✗
    FailingFor documents that are not strongly structured, as described in ISO 32000-1:2…2 violations
    For documents that are not strongly structured, as described in ISO 32000-1:2008, 14.8.4.3.5, heading tags shall be used as follows: (*) If any heading tags are used, H1 shall be the first. (*) A document may use more than one instance of any specific tag level. For example, a tag level may be repeated if document content requires it. (*) If document semantics require a descending sequence of headers, such a sequence shall proceed in strict numerical order and shall not skip an intervening heading level. (*) A document may increment its heading sequence without restarting at H1 if document semantics require it
    7.4.2-t1 For documents that are not strongly structured, as described in ISO 32000-1:2008, 14.8.4.3.5, heading tags shall be used as follows: (*) If any heading tags are used, H1 shall be the first. (*) A document may use more than one instance of any specific tag level. For example, a tag level may be repeated if document content requires it. (*) If document semantics require a descending sequence of headers, such a sequence shall proceed in strict numerical order and shall not skip an intervening heading level. (*) A document may increment its heading sequence without restarting at H1 if document semantics require it

    FailingFigure tags shall include an alternative representation or replacement text t…1 violation
    Figure tags shall include an alternative representation or replacement text that represents the contents marked with the Figure tag as noted in ISO 32000-1:2008, 14.7.2, Table 323
    7.3-t1 Figure tags shall include an alternative representation or replacement text that represents the contents marked with the Figure tag as noted in ISO 32000-1:2008, 14.7.2, Table 323
  • ✗
    FailingFor documents that are not strongly structured, as described in ISO 32000-1:2…2 violations
    For documents that are not strongly structured, as described in ISO 32000-1:2008, 14.8.4.3.5, heading tags shall be used as follows: (*) If any heading tags are used, H1 shall be the first. (*) A document may use more than one instance of any specific tag level. For example, a tag level may be repeated if document content requires it. (*) If document semantics require a descending sequence of headers, such a sequence shall proceed in strict numerical order and shall not skip an intervening heading level. (*) A document may increment its heading sequence without restarting at H1 if document semantics require it
    7.4.2-t1 For documents that are not strongly structured, as described in ISO 32000-1:2008, 14.8.4.3.5, heading tags shall be used as follows: (*) If any heading tags are used, H1 shall be the first. (*) A document may use more than one instance of any specific tag level. For example, a tag level may be repeated if document content requires it. (*) If document semantics require a descending sequence of headers, such a sequence shall proceed in strict numerical order and shall not skip an intervening heading level. (*) A document may increment its heading sequence without restarting at H1 if document semantics require it

Rule-by-rule detail
  • ✗
    FailingMissing PDF/UA marker1 violation
    The PDF didn't declare itself as following the PDF/UA accessibility standard.
    5-t1 The PDF/UA version and conformance level of a file shall be specified using the PDF/UA Identification extension schema
  • ✗
    FailingThe document catalog dictionary shall include a ViewerPreferences dictionary …1 violation
    The document catalog dictionary shall include a ViewerPreferences dictionary containing a DisplayDocTitle key, whose value shall be true
    7.1-t10 The document catalog dictionary shall include a ViewerPreferences dictionary containing a DisplayDocTitle key, whose value shall be true
  • ✗
    FailingAnnotation without alt text5 violations
    Clickable areas (links, form fields, etc.) had no description for screen-reader users.
    7.18.1-t2 An annotation (except Widget annotations or hidden annotations, or those having rectangle outside the crop-box) shall have either Contents key or an Alt entry in the enclosing structure element
  • ✗
    FailingHyperlinks without description5 violations
    Hyperlinks had no accessible text describing where they go.
    7.18.5-t2 Links shall contain an alternate description via their Contents key as described in ISO 32000-1:2008, 14.9.3

    TR element may contain only TH and TD elements
    7.2-t10 TR element may contain only TH and TD elements

    7.2-t42 Table rows shall have the same number of columns (taking into account column spans)

    FailingFont not embedded6 violations

    7.21.4.1-t1 The font programs for all fonts used for rendering within a conforming file shall be embedded within that file, as defined in ISO 32000-1:2008, 9.9

    FailingThe logical structure of the conforming file shall be described by a structur…1 violation

    FailingUntagged content3,711 violations

  • ✗
    FailingThe Catalog dictionary of a conforming file shall contain the Metadata key wh…1 violation
    The Catalog dictionary of a conforming file shall contain the Metadata key whose value is a metadata stream as defined in ISO 32000-1:2008, 14.3.2. The metadata stream dictionary shall contain entry Type with value /Metadata and entry Subtype with value /XML
    7.1-t8 The Catalog dictionary of a conforming file shall contain the Metadata key whose value is a metadata stream as defined in ISO 32000-1:2008, 14.3.2. The metadata stream dictionary shall contain entry Type with value /Metadata and entry Subtype with value /XML
  • ✗
    FailingAnnotation without alt text5 violations
    Clickable areas (links, form fields, etc.) had no description for screen-reader users.
    7.18.1-t2 An annotation (except Widget annotations or hidden annotations, or those having rectangle outside the crop-box) shall have either Contents key or an Alt entry in the enclosing structure element
  • ✗
    FailingEvery page on which there is an annotation shall contain in its page dictiona…3 violations
    Every page on which there is an annotation shall contain in its page dictionary the key Tabs, and its value shall be S
    7.18.3-t1 Every page on which there is an annotation shall contain in its page dictionary the key Tabs, and its value shall be S

  • ✗
    FailingFont CIDSet incomplete1 violation
    An embedded CID font's CIDSet didn't list all character IDs in the font.
    7.21.4.2-t2 If the FontDescriptor dictionary of an embedded CID font contains a CIDSet stream, then it shall identify all CIDs which are present in the font program, regardless of whether a CID in the font is referenced or used by the PDF or not
  • ✗
    FailingFor documents that are not strongly structured, as described in ISO 32000-1:2…2 violations
    For documents that are not strongly structured, as described in ISO 32000-1:2008, 14.8.4.3.5, heading tags shall be used as follows: (*) If any heading tags are used, H1 shall be the first. (*) A document may use more than one instance of any specific tag level. For example, a tag level may be repeated if document content requires it. (*) If document semantics require a descending sequence of headers, such a sequence shall proceed in strict numerical order and shall not skip an intervening heading level. (*) A document may increment its heading sequence without restarting at H1 if document semantics require it
    7.4.2-t1 For documents that are not strongly structured, as described in ISO 32000-1:2008, 14.8.4.3.5, heading tags shall be used as follows: (*) If any heading tags are used, H1 shall be the first. (*) A document may use more than one instance of any specific tag level. For example, a tag level may be repeated if document content requires it. (*) If document semantics require a descending sequence of headers, such a sequence shall proceed in strict numerical order and shall not skip an intervening heading level. (*) A document may increment its heading sequence without restarting at H1 if document semantics require it

    FailingFont not embedded8 violations
    A font used by the PDF wasn't embedded — it may render incorrectly on other systems.

  • ✗
    FailingFont CIDSet incomplete1 violation
    An embedded CID font's CIDSet didn't list all character IDs in the font.
    7.21.4.2-t2 If the FontDescriptor dictionary of an embedded CID font contains a CIDSet stream, then it shall identify all CIDs which are present in the font program, regardless of whether a CID in the font is referenced or used by the PDF or not
  • ✗
    FailingFigure tags shall include an alternative representation or replacement text t…1 violation
    Figure tags shall include an alternative representation or replacement text that represents the contents marked with the Figure tag as noted in ISO 32000-1:2008, 14.7.2, Table 323
    7.3-t1 Figure tags shall include an alternative representation or replacement text that represents the contents marked with the Figure tag as noted in ISO 32000-1:2008, 14.7.2, Table 323
  • ✗
    FailingFor documents that are not strongly structured, as described in ISO 32000-1:2…2 violations
    For documents that are not strongly structured, as described in ISO 32000-1:2008, 14.8.4.3.5, heading tags shall be used as follows: (*) If any heading tags are used, H1 shall be the first. (*) A document may use more than one instance of any specific tag level. For example, a tag level may be repeated if document content requires it. (*) If document semantics require a descending sequence of headers, such a sequence shall proceed in strict numerical order and shall not skip an intervening heading level. (*) A document may increment its heading sequence without restarting at H1 if document semantics require it
    7.4.2-t1 For documents that are not strongly structured, as described in ISO 32000-1:2008, 14.8.4.3.5, heading tags shall be used as follows: (*) If any heading tags are used, H1 shall be the first. (*) A document may use more than one instance of any specific tag level. For example, a tag level may be repeated if document content requires it. (*) If document semantics require a descending sequence of headers, such a sequence shall proceed in strict numerical order and shall not skip an intervening heading level. (*) A document may increment its heading sequence without restarting at H1 if document semantics require it

    7.1-t8 The Catalog dictionary of a conforming file shall contain the Metadata key whose value is a metadata stream as defined in ISO 32000-1:2008, 14.3.2. The metadata stream dictionary shall contain entry Type with value /Metadata and entry Subtype with value /XML

  • ✗
    FailingFigure tags shall include an alternative representation or replacement text t…1 violation
    Figure tags shall include an alternative representation or replacement text that represents the contents marked with the Figure tag as noted in ISO 32000-1:2008, 14.7.2, Table 323
    7.3-t1 Figure tags shall include an alternative representation or replacement text that represents the contents marked with the Figure tag as noted in ISO 32000-1:2008, 14.7.2, Table 323
  • ✗
    FailingFor documents that are not strongly structured, as described in ISO 32000-1:2…2 violations
    For documents that are not strongly structured, as described in ISO 32000-1:2008, 14.8.4.3.5, heading tags shall be used as follows: (*) If any heading tags are used, H1 shall be the first. (*) A document may use more than one instance of any specific tag level. For example, a tag level may be repeated if document content requires it. (*) If document semantics require a descending sequence of headers, such a sequence shall proceed in strict numerical order and shall not skip an intervening heading level. (*) A document may increment its heading sequence without restarting at H1 if document semantics require it
    7.4.2-t1 For documents that are not strongly structured, as described in ISO 32000-1:2008, 14.8.4.3.5, heading tags shall be used as follows: (*) If any heading tags are used, H1 shall be the first. (*) A document may use more than one instance of any specific tag level. For example, a tag level may be repeated if document content requires it. (*) If document semantics require a descending sequence of headers, such a sequence shall proceed in strict numerical order and shall not skip an intervening heading level. (*) A document may increment its heading sequence without restarting at H1 if document semantics require it

  • ✗
    FailingFont CIDSet incomplete1 violation
    An embedded CID font's CIDSet didn't list all character IDs in the font.
    7.21.4.2-t2 If the FontDescriptor dictionary of an embedded CID font contains a CIDSet stream, then it shall identify all CIDs which are present in the font program, regardless of whether a CID in the font is referenced or used by the PDF or not
  • ✗
    FailingFigure tags shall include an alternative representation or replacement text t…1 violation
    Figure tags shall include an alternative representation or replacement text that represents the contents marked with the Figure tag as noted in ISO 32000-1:2008, 14.7.2, Table 323
    7.3-t1 Figure tags shall include an alternative representation or replacement text that represents the contents marked with the Figure tag as noted in ISO 32000-1:2008, 14.7.2, Table 323
  • ✗
    FailingFor documents that are not strongly structured, as described in ISO 32000-1:2…2 violations
    For documents that are not strongly structured, as described in ISO 32000-1:2008, 14.8.4.3.5, heading tags shall be used as follows: (*) If any heading tags are used, H1 shall be the first. (*) A document may use more than one instance of any specific tag level. For example, a tag level may be repeated if document content requires it. (*) If document semantics require a descending sequence of headers, such a sequence shall proceed in strict numerical order and shall not skip an intervening heading level. (*) A document may increment its heading sequence without restarting at H1 if document semantics require it
    7.4.2-t1 For documents that are not strongly structured, as described in ISO 32000-1:2008, 14.8.4.3.5, heading tags shall be used as follows: (*) If any heading tags are used, H1 shall be the first. (*) A document may use more than one instance of any specific tag level. For example, a tag level may be repeated if document content requires it. (*) If document semantics require a descending sequence of headers, such a sequence shall proceed in strict numerical order and shall not skip an intervening heading level. (*) A document may increment its heading sequence without restarting at H1 if document semantics require it

  • ✗
    FailingFor documents that are not strongly structured, as described in ISO 32000-1:2…2 violations
    For documents that are not strongly structured, as described in ISO 32000-1:2008, 14.8.4.3.5, heading tags shall be used as follows: (*) If any heading tags are used, H1 shall be the first. (*) A document may use more than one instance of any specific tag level. For example, a tag level may be repeated if document content requires it. (*) If document semantics require a descending sequence of headers, such a sequence shall proceed in strict numerical order and shall not skip an intervening heading level. (*) A document may increment its heading sequence without restarting at H1 if document semantics require it
    7.4.2-t1 For documents that are not strongly structured, as described in ISO 32000-1:2008, 14.8.4.3.5, heading tags shall be used as follows: (*) If any heading tags are used, H1 shall be the first. (*) A document may use more than one instance of any specific tag level. For example, a tag level may be repeated if document content requires it. (*) If document semantics require a descending sequence of headers, such a sequence shall proceed in strict numerical order and shall not skip an intervening heading level. (*) A document may increment its heading sequence without restarting at H1 if document semantics require it
  • ✗
    FailingTable headers without scope9 violations
    Table-header cells didn't say whether they apply to a row or a column.
    7.5-t1 If the table's structure is not determinable via Headers and IDs, then structure elements of type TH shall have a Scope attribute

    7.1-t8 The Catalog dictionary of a conforming file shall contain the Metadata key whose value is a metadata stream as defined in ISO 32000-1:2008, 14.3.2. The metadata stream dictionary shall contain entry Type with value /Metadata and entry Subtype with value /XML
  • ✗
    FailingAnnotation without alt text47 violations
    Clickable areas (links, form fields, etc.) had no description for screen-reader users.
    7.18.1-t2 An annotation (except Widget annotations or hidden annotations, or those having rectangle outside the crop-box) shall have either Contents key or an Alt entry in the enclosing structure element
  • ✗
    FailingUntagged hyperlinks33 violations

    7.18.5-t1 Links shall be tagged according to ISO 32000-1:2008, 14.8.4.4.2, Link Element

    FailingMissing document title1 violation

    Newly failingEmbedded fonts shall define all glyphs referenced for rendering within the co…2 violations

A font referenced solely in text rendering mode 3 is not rendered and is thus exempt from the requirements that impact the visual representation of the glyphs of a font. In all cases for TrueType fonts that are to be rendered, character codes shall be able to be mapped to glyphs according to ISO 32000-1:2008, 9.6.6.4 without the use of a non-standard mapping chosen by the conforming processor
    7.21.4.1-t2 Embedded fonts shall define all glyphs referenced for rendering within the conforming file. A font referenced solely in text rendering mode 3 is not rendered and is thus exempt from the requirements that impact the visual representation of the glyphs of a font. In all cases for TrueType fonts that are to be rendered, character codes shall be able to be mapped to glyphs according to ISO 32000-1:2008, 9.6.6.4 without the use of a non-standard mapping chosen by the conforming processor
  • ✗
    Newly failingFont glyph widths inconsistent2 violations
    A font's declared character widths didn't match what was in the embedded font file.
    7.21.5-t1 For every font embedded in a conforming file and used for rendering, the glyph width information in the font dictionary and in the embedded font program shall be consistent

  • ✓
    FixedNon-standard tag without mapping4 violations cleared
    Custom structure tags were used without being mapped to a standard equivalent.
    7.1-t5 All non-standard structure types shall be mapped to the nearest functionally equivalent standard type, as defined in ISO 32000-1:2008, 14.8.4, in the role map dictionary of the structure tree root. This mapping may be indirect; within the role map a non-standard type can map directly to another non-standard type, but eventually the mapping shall terminate at a standard type

    FixedAnnotation without alt text9 violations cleared

    FixedHyperlinks without description9 violations cleared

  • ✓
    FixedFont not embedded1 violation cleared
    A font used by the PDF wasn't embedded — it may render incorrectly on other systems.
    7.21.4.1-t1 The font programs for all fonts used for rendering within a conforming file shall be embedded within that file, as defined in ISO 32000-1:2008, 9.9

    FixedNon-standard tag without mapping6 violations cleared
    Custom structure tags were used without being mapped to a standard equivalent.
    7.1-t5 All non-standard structure types shall be mapped to the nearest functionally equivalent standard type, as defined in ISO 32000-1:2008, 14.8.4, in the role map dictionary of the structure tree root. This mapping may be indirect; within the role map a non-standard type can map directly to another non-standard type, but eventually the mapping shall terminate at a standard type

    FixedFor documents that are not strongly structured, as described in ISO 32000-1:2…1 violation cleared
    For documents that are not strongly structured, as described in ISO 32000-1:2008, 14.8.4.3.5, heading tags shall be used as follows: (*) If any heading tags are used, H1 shall be the first. (*) A document may use more than one instance of any specific tag level. For example, a tag level may be repeated if document content requires it. (*) If document semantics require a descending sequence of headers, such a sequence shall proceed in strict numerical order and shall not skip an intervening heading level. (*) A document may increment its heading sequence without restarting at H1 if document semantics require it
    7.4.2-t1 For documents that are not strongly structured, as described in ISO 32000-1:2008, 14.8.4.3.5, heading tags shall be used as follows: (*) If any heading tags are used, H1 shall be the first. (*) A document may use more than one instance of any specific tag level. For example, a tag level may be repeated if document content requires it. (*) If document semantics require a descending sequence of headers, such a sequence shall proceed in strict numerical order and shall not skip an intervening heading level. (*) A document may increment its heading sequence without restarting at H1 if document semantics require it
  • ✓
    FixedTable headers without scope3 violations cleared
    Table-header cells didn't say whether they apply to a row or a column.
    7.5-t1 If the table's structure is not determinable via Headers and IDs, then structure elements of type TH shall have a Scope attribute

    FixedNon-standard tag without mapping5 violations cleared
    Custom structure tags were used without being mapped to a standard equivalent.
    7.1-t5 All non-standard structure types shall be mapped to the nearest functionally equivalent standard type, as defined in ISO 32000-1:2008, 14.8.4, in the role map dictionary of the structure tree root. This mapping may be indirect; within the role map a non-standard type can map directly to another non-standard type, but eventually the mapping shall terminate at a standard type

    7.3-t1 Figure tags shall include an alternative representation or replacement text that represents the contents marked with the Figure tag as noted in ISO 32000-1:2008, 14.7.2, Table 323
  • ✓
    FixedFor documents that are not strongly structured, as described in ISO 32000-1:2…1 violation cleared
    For documents that are not strongly structured, as described in ISO 32000-1:2008, 14.8.4.3.5, heading tags shall be used as follows: (*) If any heading tags are used, H1 shall be the first. (*) A document may use more than one instance of any specific tag level. For example, a tag level may be repeated if document content requires it. (*) If document semantics require a descending sequence of headers, such a sequence shall proceed in strict numerical order and shall not skip an intervening heading level. (*) A document may increment its heading sequence without restarting at H1 if document semantics require it
    7.4.2-t1 For documents that are not strongly structured, as described in ISO 32000-1:2008, 14.8.4.3.5, heading tags shall be used as follows: (*) If any heading tags are used, H1 shall be the first. (*) A document may use more than one instance of any specific tag level. For example, a tag level may be repeated if document content requires it. (*) If document semantics require a descending sequence of headers, such a sequence shall proceed in strict numerical order and shall not skip an intervening heading level. (*) A document may increment its heading sequence without restarting at H1 if document semantics require it
  • ✓
    FixedTable headers without scope8 violations cleared
    Table-header cells didn't say whether they apply to a row or a column.
    7.5-t1 If the table's structure is not determinable via Headers and IDs, then structure elements of type TH shall have a Scope attribute

    FixedFor documents that are not strongly structured, as described in ISO 32000-1:2…1 violation cleared
    For documents that are not strongly structured, as described in ISO 32000-1:2008, 14.8.4.3.5, heading tags shall be used as follows: (*) If any heading tags are used, H1 shall be the first. (*) A document may use more than one instance of any specific tag level. For example, a tag level may be repeated if document content requires it. (*) If document semantics require a descending sequence of headers, such a sequence shall proceed in strict numerical order and shall not skip an intervening heading level. (*) A document may increment its heading sequence without restarting at H1 if document semantics require it
    7.4.2-t1 For documents that are not strongly structured, as described in ISO 32000-1:2008, 14.8.4.3.5, heading tags shall be used as follows: (*) If any heading tags are used, H1 shall be the first. (*) A document may use more than one instance of any specific tag level. For example, a tag level may be repeated if document content requires it. (*) If document semantics require a descending sequence of headers, such a sequence shall proceed in strict numerical order and shall not skip an intervening heading level. (*) A document may increment its heading sequence without restarting at H1 if document semantics require it
  • ✓
    FixedTable headers without scope4 violations cleared
    Table-header cells didn't say whether they apply to a row or a column.
    7.5-t1 If the table's structure is not determinable via Headers and IDs, then structure elements of type TH shall have a Scope attribute

  • ✓
    FixedFor documents that are not strongly structured, as described in ISO 32000-1:2…2 violations cleared
    For documents that are not strongly structured, as described in ISO 32000-1:2008, 14.8.4.3.5, heading tags shall be used as follows: (*) If any heading tags are used, H1 shall be the first. (*) A document may use more than one instance of any specific tag level. For example, a tag level may be repeated if document content requires it. (*) If document semantics require a descending sequence of headers, such a sequence shall proceed in strict numerical order and shall not skip an intervening heading level. (*) A document may increment its heading sequence without restarting at H1 if document semantics require it
    7.4.2-t1 For documents that are not strongly structured, as described in ISO 32000-1:2008, 14.8.4.3.5, heading tags shall be used as follows: (*) If any heading tags are used, H1 shall be the first. (*) A document may use more than one instance of any specific tag level. For example, a tag level may be repeated if document content requires it. (*) If document semantics require a descending sequence of headers, such a sequence shall proceed in strict numerical order and shall not skip an intervening heading level. (*) A document may increment its heading sequence without restarting at H1 if document semantics require it

  • ✓
    FixedFor documents that are not strongly structured, as described in ISO 32000-1:2…2 violations cleared
    For documents that are not strongly structured, as described in ISO 32000-1:2008, 14.8.4.3.5, heading tags shall be used as follows: (*) If any heading tags are used, H1 shall be the first. (*) A document may use more than one instance of any specific tag level. For example, a tag level may be repeated if document content requires it. (*) If document semantics require a descending sequence of headers, such a sequence shall proceed in strict numerical order and shall not skip an intervening heading level. (*) A document may increment its heading sequence without restarting at H1 if document semantics require it
    7.4.2-t1 For documents that are not strongly structured, as described in ISO 32000-1:2008, 14.8.4.3.5, heading tags shall be used as follows: (*) If any heading tags are used, H1 shall be the first. (*) A document may use more than one instance of any specific tag level. For example, a tag level may be repeated if document content requires it. (*) If document semantics require a descending sequence of headers, such a sequence shall proceed in strict numerical order and shall not skip an intervening heading level. (*) A document may increment its heading sequence without restarting at H1 if document semantics require it

  • ✓
    FixedFont CIDSet incomplete1 violation cleared
    An embedded CID font's CIDSet didn't list all character IDs in the font.
    7.21.4.2-t2 If the FontDescriptor dictionary of an embedded CID font contains a CIDSet stream, then it shall identify all CIDs which are present in the font program, regardless of whether a CID in the font is referenced or used by the PDF or not
  • ✓
    FixedFor documents that are not strongly structured, as described in ISO 32000-1:2…2 violations cleared
    For documents that are not strongly structured, as described in ISO 32000-1:2008, 14.8.4.3.5, heading tags shall be used as follows: (*) If any heading tags are used, H1 shall be the first. (*) A document may use more than one instance of any specific tag level. For example, a tag level may be repeated if document content requires it. (*) If document semantics require a descending sequence of headers, such a sequence shall proceed in strict numerical order and shall not skip an intervening heading level. (*) A document may increment its heading sequence without restarting at H1 if document semantics require it
    7.4.2-t1 For documents that are not strongly structured, as described in ISO 32000-1:2008, 14.8.4.3.5, heading tags shall be used as follows: (*) If any heading tags are used, H1 shall be the first. (*) A document may use more than one instance of any specific tag level. For example, a tag level may be repeated if document content requires it. (*) If document semantics require a descending sequence of headers, such a sequence shall proceed in strict numerical order and shall not skip an intervening heading level. (*) A document may increment its heading sequence without restarting at H1 if document semantics require it

  • ✓
    FixedFont CIDSet incomplete2 violations cleared
    An embedded CID font's CIDSet didn't list all character IDs in the font.
    7.21.4.2-t2 If the FontDescriptor dictionary of an embedded CID font contains a CIDSet stream, then it shall identify all CIDs which are present in the font program, regardless of whether a CID in the font is referenced or used by the PDF or not
  • ✓
    FixedFigure tags shall include an alternative representation or replacement text t…1 violation cleared
    Figure tags shall include an alternative representation or replacement text that represents the contents marked with the Figure tag as noted in ISO 32000-1:2008, 14.7.2, Table 323
    7.3-t1 Figure tags shall include an alternative representation or replacement text that represents the contents marked with the Figure tag as noted in ISO 32000-1:2008, 14.7.2, Table 323
  • ✓
    FixedFor documents that are not strongly structured, as described in ISO 32000-1:2…2 violations cleared
    For documents that are not strongly structured, as described in ISO 32000-1:2008, 14.8.4.3.5, heading tags shall be used as follows: (*) If any heading tags are used, H1 shall be the first. (*) A document may use more than one instance of any specific tag level. For example, a tag level may be repeated if document content requires it. (*) If document semantics require a descending sequence of headers, such a sequence shall proceed in strict numerical order and shall not skip an intervening heading level. (*) A document may increment its heading sequence without restarting at H1 if document semantics require it
    7.4.2-t1 For documents that are not strongly structured, as described in ISO 32000-1:2008, 14.8.4.3.5, heading tags shall be used as follows: (*) If any heading tags are used, H1 shall be the first. (*) A document may use more than one instance of any specific tag level. For example, a tag level may be repeated if document content requires it. (*) If document semantics require a descending sequence of headers, such a sequence shall proceed in strict numerical order and shall not skip an intervening heading level. (*) A document may increment its heading sequence without restarting at H1 if document semantics require it

    FixedFont CIDSet incomplete1 violation cleared

    7.21.4.2-t2 If the FontDescriptor dictionary of an embedded CID font contains a CIDSet stream, then it shall identify all CIDs which are present in the font program, regardless of whether a CID in the font is referenced or used by the PDF or not
  • ✓
    FixedFor documents that are not strongly structured, as described in ISO 32000-1:2…2 violations cleared
    For documents that are not strongly structured, as described in ISO 32000-1:2008, 14.8.4.3.5, heading tags shall be used as follows: (*) If any heading tags are used, H1 shall be the first. (*) A document may use more than one instance of any specific tag level. For example, a tag level may be repeated if document content requires it. (*) If document semantics require a descending sequence of headers, such a sequence shall proceed in strict numerical order and shall not skip an intervening heading level. (*) A document may increment its heading sequence without restarting at H1 if document semantics require it
    7.4.2-t1 For documents that are not strongly structured, as described in ISO 32000-1:2008, 14.8.4.3.5, heading tags shall be used as follows: (*) If any heading tags are used, H1 shall be the first. (*) A document may use more than one instance of any specific tag level. For example, a tag level may be repeated if document content requires it. (*) If document semantics require a descending sequence of headers, such a sequence shall proceed in strict numerical order and shall not skip an intervening heading level. (*) A document may increment its heading sequence without restarting at H1 if document semantics require it

    FixedFigure tags shall include an alternative representation or replacement text t…1 violation cleared
    Figure tags shall include an alternative representation or replacement text that represents the contents marked with the Figure tag as noted in ISO 32000-1:2008, 14.7.2, Table 323
    7.3-t1 Figure tags shall include an alternative representation or replacement text that represents the contents marked with the Figure tag as noted in ISO 32000-1:2008, 14.7.2, Table 323
  • ✓
    FixedFor documents that are not strongly structured, as described in ISO 32000-1:2…2 violations cleared
    For documents that are not strongly structured, as described in ISO 32000-1:2008, 14.8.4.3.5, heading tags shall be used as follows: (*) If any heading tags are used, H1 shall be the first. (*) A document may use more than one instance of any specific tag level. For example, a tag level may be repeated if document content requires it. (*) If document semantics require a descending sequence of headers, such a sequence shall proceed in strict numerical order and shall not skip an intervening heading level. (*) A document may increment its heading sequence without restarting at H1 if document semantics require it
    7.4.2-t1 For documents that are not strongly structured, as described in ISO 32000-1:2008, 14.8.4.3.5, heading tags shall be used as follows: (*) If any heading tags are used, H1 shall be the first. (*) A document may use more than one instance of any specific tag level. For example, a tag level may be repeated if document content requires it. (*) If document semantics require a descending sequence of headers, such a sequence shall proceed in strict numerical order and shall not skip an intervening heading level. (*) A document may increment its heading sequence without restarting at H1 if document semantics require it
  • ✓
    FixedTable headers without scope9 violations cleared
    Table-header cells didn't say whether they apply to a row or a column.
    7.5-t1 If the table's structure is not determinable via Headers and IDs, then structure elements of type TH shall have a Scope attribute

  • ✓
    FixedMissing PDF/UA marker1 violation cleared
    The PDF didn't declare itself as following the PDF/UA accessibility standard.

    FixedThe document catalog dictionary shall include a ViewerPreferences dictionary …1 violation cleared
    The document catalog dictionary shall include a ViewerPreferences dictionary containing a DisplayDocTitle key, whose value shall be true
    7.1-t10 The document catalog dictionary shall include a ViewerPreferences dictionary containing a DisplayDocTitle key, whose value shall be true
  • ✓
    FixedFigure tags shall include an alternative representation or replacement text t…8 violations cleared
    Figure tags shall include an alternative representation or replacement text that represents the contents marked with the Figure tag as noted in ISO 32000-1:2008, 14.7.2, Table 323
    7.3-t1 Figure tags shall include an alternative representation or replacement text that represents the contents marked with the Figure tag as noted in ISO 32000-1:2008, 14.7.2, Table 323

Rule-by-rule detail
  • ✓
    FixedThe document catalog dictionary shall include a ViewerPreferences dictionary …1 violation cleared
    The document catalog dictionary shall include a ViewerPreferences dictionary containing a DisplayDocTitle key, whose value shall be true
    7.1-t10 The document catalog dictionary shall include a ViewerPreferences dictionary containing a DisplayDocTitle key, whose value shall be true
  • ✓
    FixedThe Catalog dictionary of a conforming file shall contain the Metadata key wh…1 violation cleared
    The Catalog dictionary of a conforming file shall contain the Metadata key whose value is a metadata stream as defined in ISO 32000-1:2008, 14.3.2. The metadata stream dictionary shall contain entry Type with value /Metadata and entry Subtype with value /XML
    7.1-t8 The Catalog dictionary of a conforming file shall contain the Metadata key whose value is a metadata stream as defined in ISO 32000-1:2008, 14.3.2. The metadata stream dictionary shall contain entry Type with value /Metadata and entry Subtype with value /XML
  • ✓
    FixedAnnotation without alt text5 violations cleared
    Clickable areas (links, form fields, etc.) had no description for screen-reader users.
    7.18.1-t2 An annotation (except Widget annotations or hidden annotations, or those having rectangle outside the crop-box) shall have either Contents key or an Alt entry in the enclosing structure element
  • ✓
    FixedHyperlinks without description5 violations cleared
    Hyperlinks had no accessible text describing where they go.
    7.18.5-t2 Links shall contain an alternate description via their Contents key as described in ISO 32000-1:2008, 14.9.3
  • ✓
    FixedL element may contain only L, LI and Caption elements1 violation cleared
    L element may contain only L, LI and Caption elements
    7.2-t19 L element may contain only L, LI and Caption elements
  • ✓
    FixedFont not embedded4 violations cleared
    A font used by the PDF wasn't embedded — it may render incorrectly on other systems.
    7.21.4.1-t1 The font programs for all fonts used for rendering within a conforming file shall be embedded within that file, as defined in ISO 32000-1:2008, 9.9
  • ✓
    FixedFigure tags shall include an alternative representation or replacement text t…6 violations cleared
    Figure tags shall include an alternative representation or replacement text that represents the contents marked with the Figure tag as noted in ISO 32000-1:2008, 14.7.2, Table 323
    7.3-t1 Figure tags shall include an alternative representation or replacement text that represents the contents marked with the Figure tag as noted in ISO 32000-1:2008, 14.7.2, Table 323

    FixedThe document catalog dictionary shall include a ViewerPreferences dictionary …1 violation cleared

  • ✓
    FixedThe logical structure of the conforming file shall be described by a structur…1 violation cleared

    FixedUntagged content7 violations cleared
    Visible text or graphics weren't labelled — invisible to screen readers.
    7.1-t3 Content shall be marked as Artifact or tagged as real content

    FixedMissing document title1 violation cleared
    The PDF had no human-readable title in its metadata.

About this report

Each PDF was checked against the PDF/UA-1 (ISO 14289-1) standard. A passing PDF meets the requirements that make it usable by people who rely on screen readers.

How remediation works

Each PDF goes through a seven-step pipeline that rebuilds the parts of the file screen readers depend on, without changing what the document looks like:

  1. Pre-checks. Clean up technical obstacles that would block tagging: optional-content layer names, embedded-file specifications, legacy XFA-form data, and broken XObject references.
  2. Fonts. Embed every font used by the document and make sure each font's character data is consistent — screen readers depend on this to translate glyphs back to text. Symbol fonts (Wingdings, ZapfDingbats, Symbol) get explicit Unicode mappings synthesised. Any font that can't be repaired aborts the pipeline rather than silently producing a non-conforming file.
  3. Strip the slate. Remove all existing accessibility tags and marked-content operators. Existing tags are often partial, malformed, or use non-standard types; rebuilding from scratch is more reliable than patching an unknown tree.
  4. Catalog metadata. Mark the document as tagged, set the document language, and tell PDF readers to display the document's title instead of the filename.
  5. XMP metadata. Write a complete metadata stream declaring the file as following the accessibility standard, with a real title and a modification date.
  6. Structure tree. Analyse the document's content semantically — headings, paragraphs, lists, tables, figures, reading order — and build a structure tree. Every visible element is labelled either as real content (with its semantic role) or as decoration to be skipped. The content streams are rewritten so the tags line up with what the renderer actually paints.
  7. Annotations. Wire up links, form fields, and other clickable areas with descriptions that screen-reader users can hear. Printer-only annotations are removed. Tab order is set to follow the document structure.

The accessibility check above runs on the original PDF and on the rebuilt PDF, then compares them rule by rule.
